key_points: Hospital Hill Apartments on Troost: safe inside, but don't park on the street
Oak Hall singles are decent-sized but isolated; roommates advised for social life
Hospital Hill closed summer 2026 for FIFA, displacing students
key_quotes: Apartments safe, do not park on the street (they have a garage). People break windows for sport.
I'd give you the advice of getting a dorm with other people, you don't want to be the one on your floor never conversing with people.
Hospital Hill Apartments will be closed to students Summer 2026 to host FIFA.

key_points: UMKC is a commuter school — traditional campus social life is weak or non-existent
Transfer students and older students actively struggle to make friends
KU is explicitly recommended over UMKC for anyone wanting a typical college experience
key_quotes: If tuition isn't an issue and you want the typical college experience, I'd recommend KU over UMKC. UMKC does not offer the same kind of campus life.
At KU you'll make friends, have active clubs and get to perform at the football games.
I'm new to UMKC as a transfer student and I'm looking for new friends to hang out with!!

key_points: Sodexo operates campus dining and has faced wage violation complaints below minimum wage
Einstein Bros and a student union area are the main on-campus food options
Off-campus options like Whole Foods and Crow Coffee frequently recommended over dining hall
key_quotes: UMKC is offering shifts on a gig platform, paying less than minimum wage. 23 days straight. Shame on whoever did this.
As someone who's worked there before, don't do it. It's not worth it.
The library, Whole Foods, and Crow Coffee are all options.

key_points: No direct mentions of counseling services quality in student posts — a telling silence
Older and transfer students explicitly report isolation and difficulty connecting socially
Financial stress from surprise aid cuts is a recurring theme affecting student wellbeing
key_quotes: I'm an older student, 31F, and desperately want to find a workout partner. Gyms intimidate me.
I chose UMKC for affordability but the tuition has now been raised twice and my scholarships haven't changed.
It happened to me almost every single semester as an undergraduate for 4.5 years — they'd allocate all of my financial aid at the beginning of the year, then claw it back.

key_points: Surprise mid-semester aid cuts happen repeatedly with no clear explanation given
Roo Advantage scholarship helps tuition-only students but is largely worthless for on-campus residents
Tuition raised twice while scholarships stayed flat — affordability promise eroding
key_quotes: Came home Friday to a message saying my Financial Aid offer has changed to the tune of several thousand dollars. My income and class load are still the same.
I chose UMKC for affordability but the tuition has now been raised twice and my scholarships haven't changed.
It happened to me almost every single semester as an undergraduate for 4.5 years.

key_points: 6-year BA/MD program is the flagship draw — nationally recognized and competitively admissioned
Dentistry and pharmacy programs have strong reputations; engineering is research-focused but behind S&T
Studio art and some smaller programs are being cut or gutted — classes frequently not offered
key_quotes: For Dentistry UMKC is a great school and has a wonderful reputation in the field.
Most of the classes listed on the course catalog have only one session per semester, if that, are scarcely offered, or not offered at all. Ceramics isn't even offered.
UMKC's engineering department has transitioned from an academic school to a research school.

key_points: MyRoo portal constantly broken; students told to use Pathway instead with no proactive notice
Advising described as unreliable — one student says advising 'fucked my schedule'
BA/MD feedback sessions for rejected applicants go unacknowledged despite repeated follow-ups
key_quotes: MyRoo is constantly in a state of not working, so never expect something out of it.
Advising fucked my schedule in August and I want to make sure this doesn't happen again.
I signed up 2 times and emailed them and I have received no info.

key_points: Parking is chronically overcrowded for a school that calls itself a commuter university
Campus is split — Volker campus and Hospital Hill are not walkable from each other
Proximity to the Plaza, Nelson-Atkins Museum, and KC streetcar are genuine lifestyle perks
key_quotes: The parking situation at UMKC is embarrassing.
As a commuter university, parking is not good.
Every single time I get to campus there's less parking. They've been destroying the lot in front of MNLC as long as I've been here and I'm graduating this spring.

key_points: Local KC alumni network is a genuine advantage for students staying in the region
Engineering students report strong internship availability at recent career fairs
National recognition outside KC is limited unless you're in medicine or dentistry
key_quotes: I met people after college who assumed I was smart because they assumed I went there for Dentistry or Medicine.
I'm a freshman EE major and walked out with 3 summer offers.
One of the bigger draws is that a lot of alumni stay in the area so networking is easier.

key_points: 6-year BA/MD has no financial aid and costs near medical school tuition — sticker shock is real
In-state and Pell-eligible students get the best deal; out-of-state value is poor
Tuition hikes without equivalent scholarship increases are undermining the affordability pitch
key_quotes: Pro — they are getting an upgrade to the conservatory building. Con — it's extremely expensive.
A much higher cost of attendance, with no aid and close to medical school tuition for 6 years.
I chose UMKC for affordability but the tuition has now been raised twice and my scholarships haven't changed.

key_points: UMKC is a professional-track urban commuter school, not a traditional college experience
Students who know why they're there — med, dental, pharmacy — tend to be satisfied
Students seeking campus life, social scene, or broad exploration should look elsewhere
key_quotes: I absolutely loved UMKC and didn't even know it existed when I was your age — but, and this is a big but, go for what is most affordable.
It's a great school for medicine and dentistry.
If you want the typical college experience, I'd recommend KU over UMKC.

key_points: **Financial Aid:** Surprise mid-semester aid cuts hit multiple students every year with no warning
**Administration:** BA/MD rejected applicants ignored after signing up for promised feedback sessions
**Dining:** Campus food vendor Sodexo caught offering sub-minimum wage gig shifts to students
key_quotes: Came home Friday to a message saying my Financial Aid offer has changed to the tune of several thousand dollars. No idea why.
I signed up 2 times and emailed them and I have received no info [about BA/MD rejection feedback].
UMKC is offering shifts on a gig platform, paying less than minimum wage. 23 days straight.

key_points: Honors program priority enrollment is genuinely valuable — snag gen eds before they fill
UMKC dental school clinic offers near-free care to the public and students
Professor Richard Delaware's calculus lectures on YouTube are beloved worldwide and freely available
key_quotes: The early class registration perk is EXTREMELY useful those first 2 years for getting the gen eds you want.
I'm a 3rd-year dental student and currently accepting patients — fillings, extractions, root canals, crowns, cleanings. Kids under 3 receive free exams.
Richard Delaware is the BEST math teacher I have ever seen. After 12 years of high school and 4 years of college.
